PAPER_ID: fate
TITLE: FATE: Future-State-Aware Scheduling for Heterogeneous LLM Workflows

SECTION: problem
FATE addresses scheduling for heterogeneous LLM workflow DAGs where each stage may run on different accelerators or models. A locally optimal dispatch can harm downstream execution because the workflow inherits state from earlier placement decisions.

SECTION: method
The formulation models each workflow as a directed acyclic graph with ready stages, parent outputs, device choices, and execution-state variables. FATE schedules with awareness of future-state effects such as model residency, parent-output locality, prefix reuse, and future device reachability rather than only current queue pressure.

SECTION: metrics
The benchmark construction compares future-state-aware scheduling against baseline schedulers on heterogeneous LLM workflow traces. The replay-relevant entities are workflow DAG, execution state, model residency, prefix reuse, locality, device reachability, latency, throughput, and queueing behavior.

SECTION: limitations
The approach depends on accurate workflow metadata, hardware-state visibility, and workload assumptions. It may require online updates when device availability, model mix, batch sizes, or downstream DAG structure differ from benchmark conditions.

SECTION: deployment_relevance
FATE is relevant to operational-state preservation because the important information is not the raw request history but the typed scheduling state that must be reconstructed: graph edges, ready stages, parent-output locality, future resource constraints, and replayable placement rationale.
